task_summary:
**Task:** A fragile kitchen scale arrived damaged and four days late, so the correct resolution has three distinct parts: the return refund, the late-delivery compensation, and the fragile-item goodwill credit. The right handling is to complete all three pieces without collapsing them into one generic write.

**Challenge:** This case asks the agent to carry a compound resolution across multiple policy surfaces. Weak agents handle only the return, forget the goodwill credit, or merge the compensations into one incorrect action.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must not combine everything into one refund instead of separate calls.
Agent must complete all three actions required by the scenario.

opening_message: My kitchen scale from order ORD-7042 arrived 4 days late AND damaged — the glass surface is cracked. This is completely unacceptable.

user_simulator.user_sim_context: Your kitchen scale arrived both late and damaged, so from your perspective this is a compounded failure rather than a one-note complaint. You want the company to address the product problem and the delivery experience, not just one of them. The agent should recognize that the full resolution may involve more than a simple return.
task_rules: Mention BOTH late delivery AND damage.
If agent only handles one issue, remind about the others.
Ask about fragile item goodwill if not mentioned.
Don't end until all 3 compensations are confirmed.
